description: In the Northern Territory, learner and provisional drivers must maintain zero BAC while driving.
During wet weather, reduce speed and leave extra space because stopping distances increase and roads may become slippery.
You must slow to 40 km/h when passing stationary emergency vehicles displaying flashing lights.
Do not drive if fatigue, illness, alcohol, drugs or medication could impair your ability to drive safely.
Scanning ahead and checking mirrors regularly helps drivers identify hazards early and respond safely.
Allow extra room around heavy vehicles and avoid travelling in their blind spots.
Loads and trailers must be properly secured and remain within legal towing and weight limits.
A NO U-TURN sign means making a U-turn at that location is prohibited.
You must not park too close to intersections, traffic lights or pedestrian crossings unless signs allow it.
Drivers must give way to pedestrians at crossings and when turning into a road pedestrians are crossing.
Police may conduct random breath testing, and drivers must comply when directed to provide a breath sample.
School zones in the Northern Territory generally operate at 40 km/h during the times shown on signs.
At a stop sign or stop line, you must stop completely and give way before entering the intersection.
Increase your following distance in wet conditions to allow extra braking time and reduce the risk of collisions.
Avoid following trucks too closely because your visibility and stopping distance may be reduced.
Slow down and obey temporary speed limits and traffic directions in roadwork areas.
Bus lanes are reserved for permitted vehicles unless signs indicate that other vehicles may enter.
Loading zones may only be used by authorised vehicles, and penalties can apply for misuse.
